introduction: *The administrator looks up with desperate hope.*
Thank the gods! I'm Mayor Goldenbottom, and I need help.
My wife and daughter vanished...
and others have gone missing too.

options.text: I'll help. Tell me what you know.

introduction: It began with a few vagrants, then townsfolk,
then my own family... Gone.
No struggle. No ransom. Just gone.

options.text: Give me leads and I'll start searching.

introduction: Three hotspots keep coming up: the Refugee Camp,
the Hill Ruins, and the old Swamp Church.
Talk to the tavern folk - they know the area better than I do.
Meredith, Garrick, and Pete might have useful information.

options.text: What do you know about the Swamp Church?
Tell me about the Refugee Camp.

introduction: There is an old chapel out in the marsh.
Reports of lights and chanting at night.
Meredith serves travelers - she hears all the rumors.
Ask her about it.
Old and crumbling stones up on the nearby hill.
Hunters say people vanish near the old arch.
Garrick knows that area - miners used to explore up there.
Talk to him.
There is a camp by the south road.
Full of frightened people having strange dreams.
Missing campers too.
Pete hangs around the tavern - he might know how to find them.
Any news? Every hour counts.
Remember to follow the leads:
the Swamp Church, the Hill Ruins, the Refugee Camp.

options.text: These tremors seem to be getting worse.

introduction: *The Mayor's face tightens with frustration.*
The tremors. Yes. Another problem I cannot solve.
Started a few weeks ago. Getting stronger each day.
*He rubs his temples.*
I have miners telling me it is the old shafts settling.
But the timing... right when people start vanishing?
I do not believe in coincidences.

options.text: Any idea what is causing them?
Let's focus on the disappearances first.

introduction: Some say the old mines. Others blame the ruins.
*He shakes his head.*
All I know is the ground shakes, my people are afraid,
and my family is still missing.
Find out what is taking our people.
Maybe that will stop the tremors too.
*He exhales, relief breaking through the worry.*
You did it. You.. You really did it.
Redstone owes you more than coin, but take this with my thanks.
We will rebuild. Bigger and Better!
If anything else threatens Redstone
you'll hear from me.

options.text: I found strange glowing ore in the mine. [Show samples]

introduction: *The Mayor examines the glowing ore samples.*
By the gods... this is what Henrik found years ago.
The ore that made the miners uneasy.
*He looks troubled.*
Thank you for bringing this to my attention.
I will have our scholars study it carefully.
*He hands you a small pouch of coin.*
For your diligence in this matter.
*The office is empty.*
The Mayor must be at the Broken Blade Tavern.
You should speak with him there.
